description: Team allocation and dependency-aware parallelization — decide when to use teams, select roles, dispatch parallel work safely. Invoke for L-size tasks to evaluate whether team dispatch reduces wall-clock time.

results.query: I'm thinking 3 agents for this: researcher, implementer, tester. is that too many or about right for the scope?
research how other companies organize cross-functional teams in fintech
go ahead and spawn 4 parallel agents to handle these tasks — I've already decided the work breakdown
I want to do an audit + implement plan, but I'm not sure if they should be one role or two parallel agents
the team_members table is showing stale data after the last deploy. debug what's wrong
let me size this work first — small fix or big feature?
the dependency graph for this refactor has 8 phases — which can run in parallel, which must be sequential? walk me through the analysis
this L-size feature has backend changes, frontend changes, and DB migrations. Should I do this solo phase-by-phase or organize parallel work?
we have 4 modules that all need similar changes — can we parallelize, or will the changes overlap and cause conflicts?
how should we structure the engineering team — flat vs squad-based? give me multiple perspectives.
